text.title: Financial Fundamentals for Subscription Businesses Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `ops-financial-basics` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Compute LTV / CAC monthly and treat LTV:CAC < 3 as a hard gate: do not increase paid-acquisition spend until the ratio recovers. Cite both numerator and denominator in any pricing or growth decision-record.
rule.rationale: LTV:CAC ≥ 3 is the empirical threshold below which paid acquisition burns cash without compounding. Lower ratios are scaled at the founder's peril.

rule.statement: Track CAC payback period in months (CAC ÷ monthly gross profit per customer). Payback > 18 months requires a written justification (e.g. enterprise contract with 5-year retention) before continuing the channel.

rationale.source: SaaS metrics canon (Bessemer State of the Cloud)

rule.rationale: Long payback periods choke runway and compound risk if retention is over-forecast. The 18-month threshold is the conservative default for solo / early-stage.

rule.statement: Produce a monthly P&L with revenue, COGS, gross margin %, and operating expenses categorised by function (R&D, S&M, G&A). Aggregate 'expenses' lines are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Aggregated expense lines hide where cash is leaking. Functional categorisation forces visibility into hiring, marketing, and infrastructure costs separately.

rule.statement: Maintain gross margin ≥ 70% for pure SaaS or document why a lower margin is structural (services-heavy, marketplace). Margin drift below 70% over two quarters triggers a pricing or COGS review.
rule.rationale: Public SaaS comparables operate at 70-85% gross margin; sustained sub-70% margin signals either pricing weakness or unmanaged COGS.

rule.statement: Report cash runway in months at every monthly close (cash ÷ monthly net burn). Runway < 12 months requires a written plan (raise / cut / grow revenue) in the same decision-record.

rationale.source: Startup finance canon (Y Combinator default-alive framework)

rule.rationale: Runway is the only metric that determines existential risk. Reporting it monthly forces founder awareness before crisis.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
